name: Sparse Concept Channels in Frozen 3D CT Vision Encoders
summary: arXiv:2607.20993v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Large vision-language models are becoming increasingly dominant in 3D medical image interpretation, but we rarely know <i>which</i> int...
description: arXiv:2607.20993v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Large vision-language models are becoming increasingly dominant in 3D medical image interpretation, but we rarely know <i>which</i> internal units encode clinical findings or <i>where</i> that information lives in the representation. We first study this on a 3D chest vision-language model (Pillar-0) by probing its frozen...
author: Farhad Nooralahzadeh, Lea Bogensperger, Christian Bluethgen, Michael Krauthammer
